text:
🚨BITCOIN IS ABOUT TO GO PARABOLIC AGAIN

Look at every cycle:

2012 -> parabolic move
2016 -> parabolic move
2020 -> parabolic move

Every time it looks different, but the result is always the same

First, the market tops, then everyone calls it over

Then price bleeds, chops, and kills momentum

And right when people lose interest… It resets

Now look at today

- same post-top structure
- same choppy range
- same loss of momentum

Bitcoin does not move when people expect it

It moves after boredom

After doubt, after everyone stops believing

Every previous cycle ended the same way

- slow grind
- compression
- then vertical expansion

And we are sitting right there – at the exact point where previous cycles flipped

I've been watching the markets for 10+ years

Turn on notifs and subscribe – I'll keep you updated

text:
【日本株、大暴落】

本日、
日本株市場から20兆円超が消滅。

原因は単純な暴落ではなく、

・米国金利の再上昇懸念
・中東情勢の緊迫化
・円安進行
・海外勢のリスクオフ

これらが同時に重なったことで、
一気に売りが加速しています。

特に今、
機関投資家が見ているのは
「日本株が下がったか」ではなく、

次に、
どこへ資金が流れるのか。

実際ここ最近は、

株 → 金
株 → ビットコイン

への資金シフトが徐々に強まっている。

そして今のBTC、
チャート的にもかなり重要な局面です。

ここを上抜けるか、
大きく崩れるか、で

今年後半の流れがほぼ決まるレベル。

この情報を元にした
“次のBTCシナリオ分析”も出すので、

見たい人は
イイネ＋リプしておいてください。

text:
Most people will only realize what’s happening after Bitcoin is already much higher.

That’s how every major cycle works.

The crowd gets scared near bottoms.
Institutions quietly accumulate.
Then suddenly everyone pretends the move was “obvious.”

If assets like silver can explode vertically after years of consolidation, imagine what happens when global liquidity fully rotates back into Bitcoin.

The dangerous part is that most people still think they have “plenty of time.”

They usually think that right before the real expansion phase begins.

text:
"Everyone is going to run out of electricity."

That's what @elonmusk said. Craig Tindale explains why he's right and why the trillion-dollar AI boom is about to collide with physics.

Plus: where Bitcoin fits when the "rainy day" finally arrives.

🎙️ New episode with @ctindale, whose work I learned about from @LukeGromen (h/t).

text:
Most blockchains operate like a factory where every worker clocks in, shreds paper all day to prove they showed up, then clocks out.

The paper shredding is the job.

That is Bitcoin mining.

Qubic is different.

The electricity burned on this network trains AI. The compute produces a real output, not proof that electricity was spent.

Here is how the rest of the system works.

676 floor managers (called Computors) inspect every product (transaction) before completion.

For anything to leave the factory, at least 451 of them must sign off as a quality assurance check.

That is 66.7% agreement.

If a bad product slips in, the majority catches it before it reaches the door.

Every seven days, the factory resets.

Managers who did not pull their weight get replaced by ones who did.

You do not keep your seat by seniority. You keep it with measurable output.

There are no transaction fees for sending and receiving QU.

When a smart contract executes, the cost comes from the contract's own reserve, not from the user's wallet.

The factory's services do not bill the customer at the counter.

And here is the part most people miss: every time someone uses the factory's services, a small amount of its internal currency gets permanently destroyed.

The more people use it, the scarcer the currency becomes.

That is the whole system.

Workers train AI. Managers verify everything by supermajority. Seats rotate weekly. Fees are zero. Usage burns supply.

Four years running.

text:
🚨 I WARNED YOU. RIGHT BEFORE THE DUMP.

But most people ignored it.

And here it comes, the massive crash.

BTC dropped to $76K.

Over $550M longs wiped out in hours.

Many hope this is just a temporary correction.

But the macro tells a different story.

Hot PPI inflation just jumped to 6%.

The fastest pace since 2022.

That killed any hope of rate cuts.

And to make things worse, the new Fed Chair Warsh is hawkish: 

Higher-for-longer rates and balance sheet shrinking.

10-year yields above 4.5%. Capital is flowing out of risk assets.

The smart money is leaving.

Spot ETF outflows are accelerating.

The crowd is praying for a quick bounce.

They will be deeply disappointed.

This is just the beginning of the real move down.

Most people will realize it only when it's too late.

Remember, I've called every major turn for the last 10 years, including the $111K top in October.

If you missed those calls, don't worry. I'll call the next one too.

Turn notifications on. If you're not following yet, you'll understand why that was a mistake later.

2014 → 2018 → 2022 → 2026

Every cycle, this line held.

Until now.

This is the signal that the capitulation phase has begun.

For the record, I was the only one publicly calling the exact bottom at $16,000 three years ago and the top at $126,000 in October.

If you missed those calls, don’t worry. I’ll call the next one too.

Turn notifications on. If you’re not following yet, you’ll understand why that was a mistake later.

text:
🚨 WAS IST ÜBER NACHT PASSIERT?

Hier die 13 wichtigsten Krypto-Highlights:

📉🩸 Bitcoin verliert innerhalb von 3 Tagen über $5K und fällt zurück auf $76.000.
📊 Daten zeigen erstmals seit dem 21. April mehr bearish als bullish BTC-Kommentare.
🌌 Ein Astrophysiker vergleicht Bitcoin mit einem wachsenden Städtenetzwerk und prognostiziert $10 Millionen pro BTC.
🟠 Strategy tätigt letzte Woche einen massiven Bitcoin-Kauf im Wert von $2 Milliarden.
📉 Bitcoin- und Ethereum-ETFs verlieren $1.07 Milliarden und beenden ihre 6-wöchige Gewinnserie.
🔥 24h-Krypto-Liquidationen steigen auf über $672 M.
🕊️ Trump sagt, er habe einen für morgen geplanten US-Militärangriff auf Iran abgesagt.
🏛️ Cynthia Lummis warnt nach dem CLARITY-Act-Erfolg im Committee, dass noch „viel Arbeit“ bevorstehe.
✅ Minnesota erlaubt Banken und Kreditgenossenschaften künftig Bitcoin-Custody-Services. 
⚠️ Citi warnt vor einer wachsenden Quantencomputer-Gefahr für Bitcoin.
📈 Die Wahrscheinlichkeit gegen Zinssenkungen steigt weiter vor der Vereidigung des neuen Fed-Chefs.
🤷‍♂️ Seit 1930 wurde jeder neue Fed-Chef innerhalb der ersten 3 Monate von einer Aktienmarkt-Korrektur getestet.
📜 Die SEC plant eine „Innovation Exemption“ für tokenisierten Aktienhandel.

❤️ Es steckt viel Arbeit in diesen Posts! Support durch Like & Kommentar motiviert extrem :)

Institutions crashed price to $60k to shake out retail -> price bounced -> now grinding sideways to exhaust sellers -> one final breakdown incoming

That final flush is called the Spring - a sweep below $60k that makes everyone think it's over

Retail panics and sells, institutions buy everything

Then price rips to $100k+ with almost nobody positioned

The best entry of this cycle is going to feel like the worst moment to buy

Follow + notifs on, I will keep you updated!

text:
🚨 DER 20 MILLIONSTE #BITCOIN WURDE GERADE GEMINED!

Überall liest du jetzt denselben Satz. 20 Millionen gemeined, nur noch eine Million bis zum Limit, jetzt wird es eng. Das klingt nach dem großen Startschuss und ist genau deshalb die falsche Fährte.

Diese letzte Million kommt nicht auf einmal. Schau dir die Emissionskurve an. Bis zum Jahr 2040 sind über 99,6 Prozent aller Bitcoin gemeind. Was danach noch kommt, die kompletten hundert Jahre bis 2140, sind zusammen rund 41.000 Stück. Ein ganzes Jahrhundert Mining bringt weniger, als ein einziger Konzern in einem guten Quartal in seine Bilanz legt. Wer auf "die letzten Coins" wartet, wartet auf etwas, das seine Urenkel noch erleben.

Die eigentliche Knappheit ist längst passiert, unbemerkt, während alle auf den Preis gestarrt haben. Ich schaue seit Monaten nur noch auf eine Zahl.

Rechne mit mir. Bis 2140 entstehen überhaupt nur noch rund 969.000 Bitcoin neu. Mehr nicht. Das ist die gesamte Restmenge, die das Protokoll bis ans Ende seiner Tage ausgibt.

Jetzt schau, was ein einziges Unternehmen hält. Strategy sitzt auf rund 819.000 Bitcoin. Das sind etwa 85 Prozent von allem, was bis 2140 überhaupt noch gemined wird. Eine Firma. Gegen ein ganzes Jahrhundert Emission.

Und alle börsennotierten Unternehmen zusammen halten heute schon rund 1,23 Millionen Bitcoin. Mehr, als das Netzwerk in den nächsten 114 Jahren überhaupt produziert.

Lass das sacken. Die komplette Zukunfts-Produktion ist kleiner als das, was Bilanzen schon weggesperrt haben. Und das sind nur die Firmen, die es offen ausweisen müssen. Staaten, Fonds und alte Wallets, die keiner mehr aufmacht, fehlen in der Rechnung komplett.

Deshalb ist "nur noch eine Million" das schwächste Argument im Markt. Es tut so, als würden die Miner das Angebot bestimmen. Dabei entsteht der Preis dort, wo jemand bereit ist zu verkaufen - oder eben nicht. Mit der Emissionsrate hat das nichts zu tun.

Während $BTC bei rund 77.000 Dollar seitwärts zappelt, verschiebt sich im Hintergrund das Einzige, was zählt: wer hält und wer abgibt. Genau das blendet die runde Zahl aus. Die echte Frage ist, wie viel von den 20 Millionen, die schon da sind, überhaupt jemals noch den Besitzer wechselt.

Aber klar. Feiern wir lieber den großen Tag.

Du jagst ein Angebot, das längst verteilt ist. Frag dich, von wem du es eigentlich kaufen willst.

text:
𝗔 𝗵𝘂𝘀𝗯𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘄𝗶𝗳𝗲 𝘀𝘁𝗼𝗹𝗲 $𝟰.𝟱 𝗕𝗜𝗟𝗟𝗜𝗢𝗡 𝗶𝗻 𝗕𝗶𝘁𝗰𝗼𝗶𝗻 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝟲 𝘆𝗲𝗮𝗿𝘀 𝗵𝗶𝗱𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝘁. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝘄𝗶𝗳𝗲 𝘀𝗽𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝘀𝗮𝗺𝗲 𝘁𝗶𝗺𝗲 𝗽𝗼𝘀𝘁𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗿𝗮𝗽 𝘃𝗶𝗱𝗲𝗼𝘀 𝗼𝗻 𝗬𝗼𝘂𝗧𝘂𝗯𝗲 𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗵𝗲𝗿𝘀𝗲𝗹𝗳 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗖𝗿𝗼𝗰𝗼𝗱𝗶𝗹𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝗪𝗮𝗹𝗹 𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗲𝗲𝘁.

– In 2016 Ilya Lichtenstein hacked Bitfinex, one of the largest crypto exchanges in the world.

– He stole the admin credentials, logged in like an employee, and quietly moved 119,754 Bitcoin into his own wallet in a single day.

– At the time it was worth $72 million. Nobody noticed for days.

– For 6 years they tried to clean the money. Crypto mixers, fake exchange accounts, stolen identities, gold bars. NFTs, Walmart gift cards, and even Uber rides.

– They were trying to launder $4.5 BILLION using Walmart gift cards.

– Meanwhile Heather was on YouTube posting rap videos under the name Razzlekhan calling herself the Crocodile of Wall Street.

– She was also writing articles for Forbes magazine. One of them was titled "Experts Share Tips To Protect Your Business From Cybercriminals."

– The woman laundering billions in stolen Bitcoin was giving the world cybersecurity advice in Forbes.

– What they did not understand is that the blockchain records every transaction permanently and publicly.

– A DOJ analyst spent years mapping every single hop, every mixer, every fake account across the entire chain.

– One imperfect transaction from 2016 connected everything back to Ilya's real name.

– Six years of laundering unravelled from a single mistake buried in a public ledger.

– When the FBI raided their Manhattan apartment in 2022 they found the private keys to $3.6 billion hidden in a file named passport photos.

– The DOJ seized it the same day. The largest financial seizure in US government history.

– Ilya got 5 years. Heather got 18 months.

They laundered $4.5 BILLION through mixers, gold bars, and Walmart gift cards for 6 years. One imperfect transaction on a public blockchain connected everything.

"Sell a kidney if you must, but keep the Bitcoin."

"Bitcoin is a stronger form of capital preservation than your house, than a bar of gold, than a bunch of silver, than the stock, than an index. All of those things are weaker"

"You bought a sports car. Good for you. You'll be wealthy for a decade. You have $200,000, you buy Bitcoin. You'll be wealthy forever"

"Sell the thing that has a half life of seven years. Don't sell the thing that will make your children's children wealthy - but keep the kidney. Keep the kidney"

Podcast out Thursday

text:
Saylor on his expectations after buying Bitcoin:

“I didn’t expect this to work in 12 weeks. I thought maybe in 1-3 years people would stop thinking I’m an idiot… and in 10 years I’d be glad I did it.”

He also talked about the only way he lost money on Apple: trying to trade it with leverage.
He bought LEAP call options, the stock stayed down too long, the options expired… then Apple exploded higher right after.

His lesson:
If you truly have conviction, buy the underlying asset, avoid leverage, and hold it long enough for the thesis to play out.

Now he's warning about what's coming next

"Bitcoin is not even good for money laundering it's too traceable. it's a cult, first time in history we're mixing money and cult"

he says real estate created $30 trillion in fake valuation that has to come down by half. companies before this crisis weren't selling you cash flow

text:
The reason I went all-in into altcoins is quite simple: this is the final window of opportunity for excessive returns on assets.

They've been presented through the commodity and energy sectors.

Now, it's semiconductors.

The moment #Bitcoin breaks through $90-100K, Bitcoin's valuation will likely go way higher than most of us expect it to go.

That's always how markets behave. During excessive bear markets, nobody believes the markets to turn around and they undervalue the upside. 

The moment Bitcoin breaks that crucial resistance level, psychologically, the money will start to flood into the crypto markets and retail, and essentially any investor, will be looking for higher yields than Bitcoin alone.

The same old game happens again, where we'll see excessive returns in altcoins in 2027-2028. Perhaps later this year.

That ride is the ride to change your life, and that's the one I'm betting on as we're likely going to face a big financial crisis after that.

text:
The longer Bitcoin stays trading sideways or goes “down,” the more time you have to accumulate cheap sats
(Sats aka Satoshis = fractions of a Bitcoin) 

Example: 

Today
- $500 @ $76,846 gets you about 650,647 sats

February 2026
- $500 at $64,069 gets you about 780,398 sats

That’s 129,751 more sats for the same $500 at different prices

This is why I don’t care how long Bitcoin trades sideways for or when it drops  

I see cheaper sats, more purchasing power, and one step closer to freedom
